key_evidence: Common or marginal standardization shrank at least one current probability gap by >=25%.
recommended_next_step: If supported, consider whether manuscript probability curves should use common or marginal standardization in a future methodological ticket.

key_evidence: Setting-rate ranges or IMV-focused setting/site diagnostics show material heterogeneity.
recommended_next_step: If supported, consider setting-stratified or setting-interaction sensitivity models.

key_evidence: At least one broad capture/completeness proxy differs, but evidence is not specific enough to prove procedure-capture bias.
recommended_next_step: If supported, audit NIV/IMV source-field construction and coding-density definitions before changing models.

key_evidence: Selected sentinels show some capture/completeness differences, but specificity or directional consistency is insufficient.
recommended_next_step: If supported, inspect selected sentinel source fields and date-field definitions before attributing NIV/IMV gaps to physiology.

key_evidence: OR-scale agreement was not consistently closer than probability-scale agreement.
recommended_next_step: Use risk differences/RRs beside ORs when communicating procedure outcomes.

key_evidence: Timing-restricted summaries were feasible but did not show a large difference.
recommended_next_step: If timestamps become available, restrict to blood gases clearly preceding NIV/IMV.

key_evidence: At least one clinical/quantile bin had ESS <100 or weighted events <10.
recommended_next_step: If supported, avoid overinterpreting tail probability gaps and consider trimming/support annotations.

key_evidence: Dual-tested subset event rates were generated for direct review.
recommended_next_step: Compare dual-tested event-rate patterns against primary ABG/VBG gaps before attributing discordance to modality.
